tldr: Congressional trading over the recent period is heavily dominated by mega-cap tech accumulation, with massive cluster buying in names like AAPL, NVDA, and MSFT totaling tens of millions in notional value. Beyond the broad tech bid, notable idiosyncratic activity includes a massive $5M+ energy buy by Rep. Chip Roy, significant semiconductor and ride-share buys by Rep. Nancy Pelosi, and targeted medical device selling by Sen. Tina Smith.

highlights.headline: Chip Roy's Massive $5M+ Bet on AESI
highlights.detail: Rep. Chip Roy executed a massive $5.0M–$25.0M purchase of Atlas Energy Solutions (AESI) in late April, making it the largest single directional trade in the recent dataset.

highlights.headline: Pelosi Buys INTC and UBER
highlights.detail: Rep. Nancy Pelosi disclosed a $1.0M–$5.0M purchase of Intel (INTC) and a $500K–$1.0M purchase of Uber (UBER) in late May, signaling conviction in the lagging semiconductor name and the ride-share giant.

highlights.headline: Broad Conviction in Mega-Cap Tech
highlights.detail: Members are heavily accumulating mega-cap tech, led by AAPL ($32.7M notional across 27 members), NVDA ($10.6M across 17 members), and MSFT ($5.9M across 30 members).

highlights.headline: Tina Smith Exits Medical Devices
highlights.detail: Sen. Tina Smith's spouse executed multiple $100K–$250K sales of medical device makers Insulet (PODD) and DexCom (DXCM) across late April and early May.

highlights.headline: Sara Jacobs Trims Qualcomm
highlights.detail: Rep. Sara Jacobs executed two large sales of Qualcomm (QCOM) totaling up to $2M in early May, contrasting with the broader congressional buying in the semiconductor space.

highlights.headline: Secondary AI and Semi Accumulation
highlights.detail: Beyond NVDA, members are clustering into other AI and semiconductor beneficiaries, including Broadcom (AVGO), TSMC (TSM), and Palantir (PLTR), showing a broadening appetite for the sector.
